blockingIssues.description: Repo::save still builds and executes its own explicit-id UPSERT transaction (BEGIN/UPSERT/COMMIT plus post-hydrate cleanup) rather than delegating to persist_explicit_id_primitive. That leaves save on a separate persistence seam, so save, upsert_at, and create_at do not yet share one canonical low-level explicit-id primitive as the feature requires.

persist_explicit_id_primitive exists and is used by create_at/upsert_at, but the reviewed commit does not connect Repo::save to it or otherwise remove the duplicated explicit-id write owner. The feature description explicitly requires save to delegate to the same primitive instead of retaining a separate UPSERT transaction path.

`normalize_record_id_strings::<T>` recursively rewrites every nested object key named `id` using `T::storage_table()`. This is not limited to the root RecordId-backed model id that the feature is supposed to preserve. Any nested payload/object field with an `id` string under the decoded row can now be coerced into a `RecordId` for the root model table, which changes shared decode semantics beyond the targeted `Repo::get_record` seam and can mis-decode unrelated nested JSON shapes.

`deserialize_record_id_or_compat_string` now routes plain strings through `parse_record_id_or_plain_string(..., Some('_'))`, which turns arbitrary non-record-id strings into synthetic `_:` RecordIds instead of preserving the previous behavior of failing invalid strings. The feature brief says the remaining blocker is the get_record/decode reload seam for bound RecordIds; widening generic compat deserialization to accept any plain string changes broader RecordId parsing behavior without dedicated proof in this feature.

classify_db_error(&anyhow::Error) still falls back to classify_db_error_message(err.to_string()), so the new helper itself reparses raw error text instead of consuming a surfaced typed category. That means foreign-resolution branches in core/src/lib.rs are still indirectly string-driven, contrary to the feature requirement that runtime decision points stop branching on error-message strings entirely.

The repository decode seam still constructs DBError::Decode(err.to_string()) directly from serde text. This feature claimed repository, foreign-resolution, and decode seams would all adopt a narrow typed classification layer, but the repository path still relies on message text rather than a shared surfaced typed category.

Validated regression coverage still includes raw string assertions for repository/not-found behavior such as err.to_string().contains(Record not found) (also at lines 2973 and 3008). The feature description and expected behavior require regressions to consume typed categories directly rather than reparsing error text, so the adoption is incomplete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The rust-library-worker skill tells workers to converge explicit-id persistence onto one canonical primitive, but it does not explicitly tell them to verify that Repo::save itself delegates to that primitive before declaring success. This gap allowed a verification-only worker handoff to mark the feature complete even though the runtime still had a second explicit-id write owner.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Skill step 16 in C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md says to converge on one canonical low-level write primitive, while current runtime code still has persist_explicit_id_primitive at core/src/repository/mod.rs:187 and a separate Repo::save UPSERT path at core/src/repository/mod.rs:663-687. The worker transcript/handoff for session 02aa3764-624d-4377-b6a5-c9bcfbce3990 reports success without any code change.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The repo/mission guidance already states that the existing working-tree state may be authoritative, but there is no matching mission-level rule that a worker must reject feature completion when the inspected baseline still contradicts the feature contract. In this case the worker treated baseline presence plus passing tests as sufficient even though diff/code inspection still showed the required delegation missing.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Mission AGENTS at C:\Users\admin\.factory\missions\fb36f232-36a4-4e47-b2c9-192ab540f0fc\AGENTS.md says the dirty working tree is authoritative baseline context, and the 02aa3764 handoff states verification-only success because the refactor was already present. However code inspection of core/src/repository/mod.rs:663-687 shows Repo::save still has its own explicit-id UPSERT transaction path, contradicting the feature description in features.json.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The rust-library-worker skill does not explicitly describe an evidence-only or proof-only path for features whose runtime fix is already present in the authoritative baseline, so the worker had to mark a procedure deviation even though adding only the missing regression proof was the correct scoped action.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Handoff C:\Users\admin\.factory\missions\fb36f232-36a4-4e47-b2c9-192ab540f0fc\handoffs\2026-03-24T05-21-31-370Z__milestone-d-record-id-public-id-normalization-narrowing__4d3f0e95-8032-4598-a1a2-4da54a6b7045.json records followedProcedure=false because the worker verified the behavior already existed in core/src/repository/mod.rs and only added the missing regressions in core/tests/integration_db.rs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The worker intentionally skipped the skill step to add/confirm a failing test from a clean baseline because the dirty tree already contained in-scope changes. The current skill mentions authoritative dirty baselines, but it still frames tests-first as the default path and does not clearly spell out how to review and constrain already-present implementation diffs for evidence-heavy follow-up features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 7906348d-e74d-4e89-90a9-901d50e91714: skillFeedback.deviations notes step 6-7 was bypassed because the dirty working tree was treated as authoritative.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The rust-library-worker skill tells workers to read mission.md and validation-contract.md first, but the review environment instructions did not surface those files as required context for scrutiny reviewers even though the worker relied on them heavily to frame the contract split.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Skill file C:/Users/admin/appdb/.factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md step 1 requires reading mission.md and validation-contract.md; the transcript skeleton for worker 82d54cb3-8fed-4165-81b9-bfe2d7392add shows both files were read before implementation, while the scrutiny reviewer prompt only mandated AGENTS.md, services.yaml, and library files for shared-state review.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The worker marked followedProcedure false only because GitNexus was unavailable and fell back to diff review, but the rust-library-worker skill does not explicitly say how to document compliance with the repo-level GitNexus requirement when the tool is missing.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations for session 8c435011-6bd6-4edf-beb2-0d4e1fcd24d4 records the GitNexus fallback; .factory/skills/rust-library-worker/SKILL.md mentions fallback use but not how to document repo-instruction compliance in the handoff.

rejectedObservations.observation: Project instructions require GitNexus impact/detect_changes before editing and committing, but the worker environment did not expose a usable GitNexus command path, so the worker fell back to git diff/code inspection instead.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous-or-not-safe-to-apply-during-scrutiny; recorded for orchestrator review instead of direct repo update

rejectedObservations.observation: GitNexus symbol lookup was partially unreliable for helper-level symbols (`decode_stored_row_value`, `deserialize_record_id_compat`), which forced fallback to targeted diff review even though GitNexus was installed. Shared guidance notes missing `detect_changes`, but not the helper-symbol lookup mismatch, so future reviewers may lose time retrying unsupported names.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous-or-not-safe-to-apply-during-scrutiny; recorded for orchestrator review instead of direct repo update
